title: Crustal structure beneath the central Chilean subduction zone from wide-angle P-wave modelling: Seismotectonic and geodynamic implications
abstract: Wide-angle seismic modelling of P-wave data from OBS/OBH instruments deployed along profiles crossing the central Chilean subduction zone (~36°–39°S) reveals the crustal structure of the forearc, continental shelf, and incoming oceanic plate. The velocity models image the subducting Nazca plate, crustal accretionary prism, and continental basement. Results constrain the seismotectonic framework and geodynamic processes of the SPOC study area.

location.description: Central Chilean subduction zone ~36°–39°S; wide-angle P-wave velocity models from SPOC OBS/OBH profiles

processing.summary: Wide-angle P-wave modelling of SPOC OBS/OBH data from ~36°–39°S provides crustal velocity structure of the central Chilean subduction zone. Velocity models image the subducting Nazca plate, accretionary prism, and continental basement, constraining seismotectonic and geodynamic interpretations.
